title: Controlled Vocabulary: Archetype Graph Edge Meanings
description: Canonical set of edge labels used across all 15 archetype graphs. Every edge in every graph must use exactly one of these meanings. This ensures consistency and enables cross-archetype analysis.

edge_meanings.label: forces commitment
edge_meanings.definition: The transition compels the character to make an irreversible choice, closing off prior options.

edge_meanings.label: reveals truth
edge_meanings.definition: The transition exposes hidden information that reshapes the character's or audience's understanding of the situation.
edge_meanings.typical_context: Revelations, unmasking, discovery of betrayal or secret

edge_meanings.label: narrows options
edge_meanings.definition: The transition eliminates available paths, increasing pressure and focusing the character toward fewer viable choices.
edge_meanings.typical_context: Escalating danger, resource depletion, ally loss

edge_meanings.label: raises cost
edge_meanings.definition: The transition increases what the character stands to lose, making subsequent choices more consequential.
edge_meanings.typical_context: Stakes escalation, new threats to loved ones, personal risk

edge_meanings.label: reframes goal
edge_meanings.definition: The transition changes what the character is pursuing or why, shifting the story's central question.
edge_meanings.typical_context: Midpoint reversals, redefinition of success, moral recalibration

edge_meanings.label: tests resolve
edge_meanings.definition: The transition subjects the character to a challenge that measures commitment to the current path.

edge_meanings.label: grants insight
edge_meanings.definition: The transition provides the character with new knowledge, skill, or perspective needed for future action.
edge_meanings.typical_context: Mentor encounters, training, reflection, earned wisdom

edge_meanings.label: triggers crisis
edge_meanings.definition: The transition initiates a moment of maximum threat or uncertainty that demands immediate response.

edge_meanings.label: enables transformation
edge_meanings.definition: The transition creates conditions under which the character can fundamentally change in identity, belief, or capability.
edge_meanings.typical_context: Death-and-rebirth moments, moral turning points, metamorphosis

edge_meanings.label: restores equilibrium
edge_meanings.definition: The transition returns the story to a stable state, resolving the central tension (though the new equilibrium may differ from the original).

edge_meanings.label: disrupts order
edge_meanings.definition: The transition breaks an existing stable state, creating the disequilibrium that drives the story forward.

edge_meanings.label: demands sacrifice
edge_meanings.definition: The transition requires the character to give up something of genuine value to proceed.
edge_meanings.typical_context: Moral dilemmas, costly victories, trades of safety for principle

edge_meanings.label: inverts expectation
edge_meanings.definition: The transition subverts the anticipated outcome, forcing reassessment of what was assumed.
edge_meanings.typical_context: Plot twists, reversals of fortune, ironic outcomes

edge_meanings.label: escalates conflict
edge_meanings.definition: The transition intensifies the opposition between the character and the antagonist or central obstacle.

edge_meanings.label: compels return
edge_meanings.definition: The transition forces or draws the character back toward the ordinary world or a prior state, carrying new weight.
edge_meanings.typical_context: Homecoming under changed circumstances, confronting origin
